title: Satellite imaging industry’s next challenge: getting systems to talk to each other
summary: The satellite imaging industry faces challenges in achieving interoperability between systems, with executives citing lack of incentives and technical barriers. This issue hinders 'tipping and cueing' capabilities across different vendors.

title: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
summary: NASA is increasing the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions but has not detailed plans to speed up crewed lunar lander development. The agency aims to enhance its lunar exploration efforts.

title: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
summary: Starfish Space has secured a new partner for its docking demonstration mission after its original partner withdrew. The company adjusted its plans following the change in partnership.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida on March 27 for final preparations ahead of a planned launch as soon as April 1. This marks a key milestone in NASA's Artemis program, which aims to return humans to the Moon.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of two new associate members, Astrolab and Zeno Power, which focus on planetary mobility and energy solutions for extreme environments.

title: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is set to decide by June on its contributions to NASA's Gateway lunar-orbiting station, following NASA's recent decision to halt work on the project. This move comes as Europe re-evaluates its role in U.S. lunar ambitions amid shifting Artemis program priorities.

title: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
summary: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to expand production of satellite propulsion systems after securing its first major overseas commercial customer. The funding follows the company's push to grow its international presence.

title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: The Space Minds podcast features a discussion with Mark Bigham, Vice President of Defense Programs at Longshot Space Technologies Corporation, about developing a hypersonic pathway to orbit.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology has revolutionized precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring, but the 'ground truth' gap limits its effectiveness without additional data sources. The Earth observation sector benefits from lower costs and improved AI-driven analytics, yet agribusinesses still need complementary methods for accurate monitoring.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have caused concern and confusion among developing companies. The head of an industry organization has warned about the potential impact of these adjustments.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 119 payloads to orbit from California early on March 30
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 119 payloads to orbit from California early on March 30. The event will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: 'We are ready': NASA still on track to launch Artemis 2 astronauts to the moon April 1
summary: NASA remains confident in launching the Artemis 2 crewed moon mission on April 1. The mission is on track for its historic lunar journey.

title: 'Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4': Release date, plot, factions, & everything else we know about the upcoming 40K strategy game
summary: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 has been announced as the next entry in the real-time strategy series set in the Warhammer 40K universe. Details include its release date, plot, and factions, though specifics remain under wraps.

title: Kristen Stewart will play Sally Ride, the 1st American woman in space, as Amazon finally greenlights mini-series 'The Challenger'
summary: Kristen Stewart will star as Sally Ride, the first American woman in space, in Amazon's newly greenlit mini-series 'The Challenger'. This marks Stewart's television debut.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon astronauts are 'fortunate' to have a private space toilet — Apollo crews pooped in plastic bags
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts will have access to a private space toilet, a significant upgrade from the Apollo era's plastic bags. The new system marks a technological advancement for crew comfort during lunar missions.

title: 'We work for them, too': Artemis 2 moon mission is for everyone, NASA astronaut Victor Glover says (video)
summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover emphasizes that the Artemis 2 moon mission is not just about milestones but also about engaging diverse communities. He highlights the mission's broader impact beyond technical achievements.

title: 'We're all on this journey together:' Who are the Artemis 2 astronauts launching to the moon?
summary: NASA has announced the four astronauts selected for the Artemis 2 mission, which will be the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The crew includes representatives from both the U.S. and Canada.

title: 'An incredible privilege and responsibility': Artemis 2's Christina Koch is ready to become the 1st woman to fly around the moon
summary: Christina Koch is preparing to become the first woman to fly around the moon as part of NASA's Artemis 2 mission. She expressed the significance of the opportunity and the team's readiness.

title: 'It's all I think about': Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman is zeroed in on historic moon mission
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of Artemis 2, expresses his focus on the historic moon mission, aiming to execute it successfully. The Artemis 2 mission marks the first moon mission in over 50 years.

title: Making Canadian history: Artemis 2 astronaut Jeremy Hansen is ready for his epic moon mission
summary: Jeremy Hansen is set to become the first Canadian astronaut to fly around the moon as part of NASA's Artemis 2 mission. This marks a historic milestone for Canada in space exploration.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's Artemis mission, highlighting his career in spacecraft communication. His journey reflects a lifelong passion for spaceflight inspired by the Space Shuttle Program.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon for $180.4 million as part of the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, aimed at advancing lunar exploration.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface imagery of the Pacific Northwest.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's imagery spans the Pacific Northwest, demonstrating its ability to observe the surface despite adverse weather conditions.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The payloads aim to test thermal protection systems, improve in-space communications, and enhance understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has selected 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including instrument deployment and sample collection. The team will help guide astronauts in conducting critical observations and experiments on the Moon's south pole.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, the main propulsion systems engineer for NASA's Artemis II mission, shares insights into his work and passion for rocket science. His email signature includes complex equations related to rocket thrust and specific impulse.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay
summary: NASA plans to release a BAA under NextSTEP-3, Appendix E, for Project NEXUS to acquire a Ka-band backward-compatible relay service. This initiative aims to replace the aging TDRS system with an end-to-end relay service.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA unveil the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew. The suits will be worn by Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen during the upcoming Artemis II test flight.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: NASA's Artemis program marks a significant milestone as America's Rocket Factory completes preparations for crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The initiative represents a full-circle moment for the U.S. space industry, aiming to return humans to the Moon and beyond.

title: SpaceX to deliver 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission. The launch will take place from California.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the mission details.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is rapidly advancing construction at Starbase to support the upcoming Block 3 Starship launches, reflecting their push for increased launch frequency. Ground crews are actively preparing the launch pads for the next generation of Starship vehicles.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also planning launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made after the Soyuz MS-28 launch from Site 31/6.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. This marks a significant expansion in the company's space industry ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a recent development in the space industry, though specific details were not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home.'

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to engage industry in ESA programs and facilitate direct dialogue on follow-up activities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Research and Technology Centre. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use the new costing software in financial proposals for ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky current solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology, which offers stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in low Earth orbit compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman marks 100 days in office by emphasizing a renewed focus on lunar missions. The announcement signals a push for accelerated Moon exploration efforts.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them with unfulfilled commitments. Europe now faces financial and strategic setbacks due to shifting priorities.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine. This funding marks a significant milestone for the company's advancement in space propulsion technology.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low-Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), following a proposal by Administrator Jared Isaacman that left many confused. The new plan aims to redefine America's human presence in low-Earth orbit.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs) in the space industry. It highlights a new approach to space defense technology.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority. This initiative aims to address gaps in existing frameworks for missions that don't fit traditional categories.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses storable bipropellant to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activities rise.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's vision for a commercially driven lunar economy has been updated with a new plan. The revised priorities aim to enhance surface infrastructure and commercial activities on the Moon.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating a rapidly deployable end-to-end ground system. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations and improve efficiency.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in this technology.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market outlook amid volatility. Discussions focused on direct-to-direct (D2D) strategies, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discusses the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview highlights advancements in satellite communication technology.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasing efforts to secure their communications by defining sovereign space capabilities amid growing geopolitical complexities. The challenge lies in determining what sovereign space entails and its operational scope within a fragmented space infrastructure.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors are discussing the need for better financing routes, including IPOs, to support the growing commercial space sector. Venture capitalists at a SATShow panel highlighted that financing methods have not kept pace with the industry's technological advancements.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking space-collected data to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now expanding their networks to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are highlighted as key trends driving the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is emphasized.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.
